summary: 2026-07-04 18:29:18 PDT — Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/meeting-summary-history.md`. I found one blocker: persisted meeting summaries can overwrite prior summaries after an app reload because generated history IDs are not stable-unique across runtimes.

findings.title: History IDs collide after reload and overwrite prior summaries
findings.claim: `addMeetingSummary` assigns summary IDs with the module-level `newId('m')`, whose `_seq` starts at 100 on every app load. A presenter who already has a stored `m100` summary will get `m100` again for the first ended session after reopening. Because the server merge dedupes by `id` with incoming rows winning, that new row replaces the old `m100` instead of adding a new history entry, violating AC1's append requirement and AC3's 'PUT can only add history, never drop it' guarantee.
findings.suggestion: Generate collision-resistant IDs for meeting summaries, such as `crypto.randomUUID()` with a fallback, or an `endedAt` plus random suffix. Add a regression test where stored history already contains `m100` and a new append after a fresh runtime still preserves both rows.
